description: Iterative launch hardening for the inquiry-first Scottsdale kosher rental website

userStories.title: Align tracked source with the active property branding
userStories.description: As the site owner, I need the tracked source to consistently use the active property identity so the sellable v1 does not leak a competing property brand.
acceptanceCriteria: Tracked source files match the active property branding in metadata and booking fallback email copy
Frontend manifest and package metadata match the active property branding
Verify in browser that the public site title matches the active property branding
userStories.notes: Use verification-before-completion. Use playwright for browser verification. Keep scope to branding cleanup only; do not delete legacy payment files in this story.

userStories.title: Add frontend tests for inquiry forms
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need automated coverage for the contact and stay-request forms so future UI changes do not silently break the inquiry-first launch path.
acceptanceCriteria: Contact form has a passing test for successful submission state
Booking request form has a passing test for successful submission state
Tests mock the inquiry API client instead of making network calls

userStories.title: Harden launch handoff documentation for deployment
userStories.description: As the client handoff owner, I need an explicit deployment and ownership checklist so the site can move from local development to a real environment cleanly.
acceptanceCriteria: Docs include frontend hosting, backend hosting, environment variables, and Mailtrap-to-production email switch guidance
Docs clearly separate v1 inquiry-first launch from deferred payment work
Documentation references the current repo structure and command set
userStories.notes: Use verification-before-completion. Keep this story documentation-only.
